title: Fig. 1 269 Mathematics
subject: Mathematics
objective: To verify that adjacent angles of a parallelogram are supplementary
materials:
Cardboard, coloured glaze paper, set square, ruler,
pencil, colours, scissors.
procedure:
1.   Take a cardboard of convenient size and paste a light coloured glaze
paper on it.
2.   Draw a parallelogram ABCD on a paper and paste it on the cardboard.
3.   Make a trace copy of this parallelogram.
4.   Colour the angles of the parallelogram ABCD in such a way that colours
of A and C and that of B and D are same (Fig. 1).
5.   Cut the four angles from the trace copy as shown in Fig. 2.
Fig. 1   Fig. 2
observation:
In Fig. 1, length of the side AB of parallelogram ABCD = _________ cm.
Length of the side BC = ________________ cm,
Length of the side CD = ________________ cm,
Length of the side AD = ________________ cm.
In   Fig. 3,   side CD covers the side ______________,
side DA covers the side ______________.
Thus,
AB = _________,
and   BC = _________.
i.e. opposite sides of a parallelogram are equal _________ .
discussion:
This result is useful in solving many other geometrical problems and also
in the construction of   parallelograms.
1.   The parallelogram ABCD may also be cut along the diagonal
BD.
2.   This activity can be used to show that the opposite angles of a
parallelogram are equal.
270 Laboratory Manual – Elementary Stage
